methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series B → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.9x) to $120M last post money.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 4 same-sector + same-stage peers in Climate & Energy. Range: $300M - $500M.

data.narrative:
Cemvita, a Series B climate and energy technology company founded by Moji Karimi, is valued at $120M post-money following $30M in total capital raised. The target operates within a competitive peer set of 9 companies across Climate & Energy—one public (LNZA) and eight private, including Pano AI, Sage Geosystems, Reverion, and CarbonCapture Inc.

Two valuation methods triangulate a range for Cemvita. Stage-progression analysis applies a 1.9x step-up multiplier typical of Series B to Series C advancement, yielding $228M. The peer-median approach anchors to four same-sector, same-stage comparables with a median valuation of $355M (range: $300M–$500M), reflecting recent market pricing for frontier-energy businesses at similar development stages.

The synthesis of these two methods implies a valuation range of $194M to $408M, with a median of $292M. This midpoint sits above Cemvita's current $120M valuation, suggesting material upside between current and next-stage pricing. Key uncertainties include the stability of the peer set—which reflects recent rather than historical valuations—and the applicability of stage-progression multipliers in a sector where capital intensity and market timing for deployment vary significantly among competitors.
